project_type: TypeScript/JavaScript monorepo - Express server + React frontend + Capacitor Android app
build_system: Bun workspaces with Vite for frontend build. Server is plain JS ESM (no transpilation needed for server code).
test_framework: Vitest (packages/web has vitest config). Test files colocated as *.test.js or *.test.ts.
verification_instructions: 1. Run 'bun run type-check' (must pass). 2. Run 'bun run lint' (must pass). 3. Run 'bun run build' (must succeed). 4. Run 'bun run --cwd packages/web test' for unit tests.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET network mode. Bun 1.3.14 and Node >=22 available. No Docker daemon needed for this task (backend code only). Daytona SDK (@daytona/sdk) will be installed as new dependency.
contribution_requirements: Functional React components only. TypeScript strict mode. No 'any' without justification. Follow existing patterns. Keep diffs tight. Prefer early returns. Tailwind v4 for styling. Run type-check and lint before finalizing.
key_patterns: Server modules use createXxxRuntime() factory pattern returning an API object. Routes registered via registerXxxRoutes(app, dependencies). Config via env vars. Settings persisted to disk via settings-runtime. WebSocket/SSE for real-time communication. OpenCode integration managed via lifecycle.js with health checks. Session management via session-runtime.js. The server proxies OpenCode API calls to a local/remote OpenCode process.
directory_structure: packages/web/server/lib/ contains focused modules (opencode/, relay/, notifications/, terminal/, etc). Each module has a service/runtime factory and optional routes. Frontend state in packages/ui/src/sync/ and packages/ui/src/stores/. The Daytona integration will add a new packages/web/server/lib/daytona/ module following the same pattern.
